title: London System: Standard Setup
summary: Master the reliable London System, a flexible setup that avoids theory and plays solid chess.
description: The London System is one of White's most reliable openings. By playing d4, Bf4, and e3, White creates a solid position that avoids main line theory. This tutorial covers the standard setup and typical plans for both sides.

variations.sysyp: 5.Nf3 - The Nf3 London, avoiding the Jobava

objectives: Understand the London structure: why it's solid and reliable
Learn why it avoids theory: the move order is flexible
Master typical plans: e4 break, or play positionally
plans: White's plan: Build a solid position, then push e4 to attack
Black's plan: Play ...c5, ...e6, and create counterplay
The move e4 is White's main break to open the position
White should maintain central pressure and piece activity
commonMistakes: Do not think the London is boring - there are many strategic ideas
Avoid playing too passively - keep the initiative
Do not underestimate Black's activity - they have many options
White should prepare e4 carefully before pushing
completionSummary: The London System is a reliable opening. You've learned the main ideas and can navigate the position.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play d4 to control the center.
steps.coaching: The London System is one of White's most reliable openings.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black responds in the center
steps.instruction: Black plays d5, challenging White's center.
steps.coaching: Black meets the London with a solid central reply.

steps.title: The London move
steps.instruction: Move the bishop to f4, controlling e5.
steps.coaching: This is the London System! White develops the bishop early.

steps.title: Support the center
steps.instruction: Play e3 to support the d4 pawn.
steps.coaching: White strengthens the center and prepares development.

steps.title: Black challenges the center
steps.coaching: This is Black's typical break against the London.

steps.title: Support the d4 pawn
steps.coaching: White strengthens the center, preparing for development.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.coaching: Black continues development, mirroring White's setup.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nd2, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: White develops the knight, supporting e4 and c4.

steps.title: Black plays e6
steps.instruction: Black plays e6, completing the structure.
steps.coaching: Black adopts a solid pawn structure.

steps.title: Develop the other knight
steps.hint: Move the knight from g1 to f3.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.coaching: Black develops the bishop, preparing castling.
